D9 Biology L4 Analyze Reference

Firefly Luciferin — ATP-Driven Yellow-Green Flash

luciferase oxidises luciferin in the presence of ATP, Mg²⁺, and O₂; the excited oxyluciferin product emits 560 nm; rate is so linear in ATP that fireflies make superb biological ATP meters

The reaction: luciferin + ATP + O₂ → oxyluciferin* + AMP + PPi + CO₂; oxyluciferin* → oxyluciferin + photon (560 nm). Quantum yield is ~0.41 (very high). Light intensity is linear in [ATP], so the same enzyme is used in clinical assays (cell viability) and contamination tests (food safety, hospitals).

Drag the ATP level. The firefly flash intensity scales linearly; the emission stays the same yellow-green colour throughout.
